channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I Replaced My 47-Line Bash Monitoring Script with One curl Command
item.description: Every DevOps engineer has that bash script. The one monitoring cron jobs. The one nobody wants to touch. Here was mine: #!/bin/bash # cron_monitor.sh - "temporary" script from 2019...

item.title: Why Most Developers Get PDF Generation Wrong (And How to Fix It)
item.description: PDF generation seems simple until it isn't. After building a PDF API that handles thousands of documents, here are the mistakes I see developers make — and better approaches. Mistake 1: Using...

item.title: The More I Use AI, The More I Value Human Thinking
item.description: I’ve been using AI for months now. Not casually, seriously. And I’ve started noticing something uncomfortable. The Observation The more I use AI, the more efficient everything becomes. Ideas come...

item.title: Feature Flagging Strategies for Continuous Deployment: Ship Daily Without Breaking Anything

item.title: Telegram vs Email for Cron Alerts, and When Webhooks Are Better
item.description: Telegram vs email for cron alerts is not just a tooling preference. It affects how fast you notice failures, how actionable your alerts are, and whether your monitoring matches the way your team...

item.title: Claude 4.7 vs 4.6: A Data-Driven Comparison (With Benchmarks)
item.description: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.7 on April 16, 2026 — just two months after Opus 4.6. The reception has been divisive: benchmark scores hit the top of the leaderboard, but developer feedback on...

item.title: AI-900 vs Data Science Certifications: What’s the Difference?
item.description: In the evolving AI landscape, not all certifications are built for the same mission. Some are designed to introduce the terrain, while others prepare you to navigate and build within it. The...

item.title: From One Agent to Many: Building a Multi-Agent Team for Snowflake Administration (Part 2)
item.description: From One Agent to Many: Building a Multi-Agent Team for Snowflake Administration (Part 2) In Part 1, we built an Admin Agent that answers questions about Snowflake usage, credits, and query...

item.title: Azure AI Engineer vs Data Scientist: Understanding the Differences
item.description: In today’s AI-driven economy, roles are evolving faster than most job descriptions can keep up. Two of the most in-demand profiles— Azure AI Engineer and Data Scientist—often overlap in perception...

item.title: Dyson’s back with a travel-size Supersonic hairdryer
item.description: Ten years ago, Dyson kicked off the hair gadget arms race with its $400 Supersonic hairdryer. Today, it's back with a slightly smaller and cheaper travel-size version. As the name suggests, the...

item.title: How to Ground a Korean AI Agent in Real Demographics with Synthetic Personas
Tim Cook will still be Apple’s Trump whisperer
item.description: Though Tim Cook is shedding his CEO title for the role of Apple's executive chairman, it appears he'll keep one of his most important duties: that of the company's Trump whisperer. "As executive...

item.title: Anthropic takes $5B from Amazon and pledges $100B in cloud spending in return
item.description: Amazon has made another circular AI deal: It's investing another $5 billion in Anthropic. Anthropic has agreed to spend $100 billion on AWS in return.

item.title: The Lenovo Legion Go S is RAMageddon’s latest victim
item.description: You can still find the Asus Xbox Ally X and the MSI Claw 8 AI Plus for $999 and $1,049 respectively, but Lenovo's Legion Go S has seemingly given up the fight. The best version of Lenovo's 8-inch...

item.title: A Humanoid Robot Set a Half-Marathon Record in China
item.description: An autonomous robot from the company Honor ran a half marathon in 50:26, beating the human record by 7 minutes.

item.title: Google rolls out Gemini in Chrome in 7 new countries
item.description: Google is rolling out Gemini in Chrome in Australia, Indonesia, Japan, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea, and Vietnam. The company is rolling this feature out to both desktop and iOS in all...

item.title: John Ternus is taking over from Tim Cook as Apple’s CEO
item.description: The multitrillion-dollar home of the iPhone, Mac, and so many other tech gadgets is getting a new leader this fall, as Apple has announced that Tim Cook will be replaced as CEO on September 1st by...

item.title: Read Tim Cook’s letter to the Apple world as he departs as CEO
item.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook has written a letter to the community as he prepares to step down from his role in September. "This is not goodbye. But at this moment of transition, I wanted to take the...

item.title: Apple names Johny Srouji as chief hardware officer
item.description: Apple has appointed Johny Srouji as its new chief hardware officer, "effective immediately," according to an announcement on Monday. He is stepping into the shoes of current hardware engineering...

item.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook is stepping down
item.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook is stepping down and will be succeeded by John Ternus, currently Apple's senior vice president of hardware engineering. Ternus will take over as CEO and join Apple's board of...

item.title: Here’s how Amazon’s price fixing allegedly drove up prices everywhere
item.description: On Monday, California Attorney General Rob Bonta revealed the evidence of alleged price-fixing by Amazon. The state filed a request to the Supreme Court in February for a preliminary injunction to...

item.title: Silicon Valley has forgotten what normal people want
item.description: One of the most mortifying things about knowing a lot of techies is listening to them tell me excitedly about some very important discovery that they believe they have made. Recently, I ran into...

item.title: WhatsApp tests ‘Plus’ subscription that adds stickers and more for a few bucks a month
item.description: WhatsApp users are getting some new customization features - for a price. According to WABetaInfo, Meta is rolling out a WhatsApp Plus subscription to "a limited number of users," offering several...

item.title: It’s not just one thing — it’s another thing
item.description: This sentence construction ("It's not just this — it's that") has become so common in AI-generated writing that it's no longer just a clue that a piece of writing may be synthetic — it's almost a...

item.title: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) Enables RCE via Malicious GGUF Model Files
item.description: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in SGLang that, if successfully exploited, could result in remote code execution on susceptible systems. The vulnerability, tracked as...

item.title: NSA spies are reportedly using Anthropic’s Mythos, despite Pentagon feud
item.description: NSA is said to be using Anthropic's restricted Mythos AI model.

item.title: CEO and CFO suddenly depart AI nuclear power upstart Fermi
item.description: The startup, co-founded by former U.S. Energy Secretary Rick Perry, has faced headwinds with its AI campus in Texas.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Vercel Hack, Push Fraud, QEMU Abused, New Android RATs Emerge & More
item.description: Monday’s recap shows the same pattern in different places. A third-party tool becomes a way in, then leads to internal access. A trusted download path is briefly swapped to deliver malware....

item.title: Prego Has a Dinner-Conversation-Recording Device, Capisce?
item.description: The pasta-sauce company has partnered with the nonprofit StoryCorps on a device designed to record family conversations around the table and save them for all time.
